_meta.description: Paired-naming dictionary for religious sites — SEO-04, AUD-017..AUD-020 data layer. Authored 2026-05-11 per plan 04 Task 2.
_meta.convention: Names follow BBC/AP paired-on-first-reference style (PITFALLS §3.1). Hebrew + English mandatory; Arabic where relevant (contested sites, mosques, Christian sites with Arabic readership). Wikidata IDs cross-reference the schema.org sameAs field.

western-wall.notes: SEO-04: NEVER use 'Wailing Wall' (archaic, mildly pejorative). 'Kotel' acceptable in context. Commercial photography requires Western Wall Heritage Foundation permit (see PITFALLS §5.4).

temple-mount.notes: SEO-04 paired-naming on first reference: 'Temple Mount / Haram al-Sharif' (EN), 'הר הבית / אל-חרם א-שריף' (HE). AUD-019: regex catches Temple Mount without nearby Haram al-Sharif. Religion field is null (contested → neutral framing per PITFALLS §3.1).

holy-sepulchre.denomination: Christian (shared by Greek Orthodox, Roman Catholic, Armenian Apostolic, Coptic Orthodox, Syriac Orthodox, Ethiopian Tewahedo — Status Quo of 1852)
holy-sepulchre.notes: Commercial photography requires acknowledgment; restrictedSiteAcknowledgment required in photo-credits.json (IMG-05). Six denominations share access via the Status Quo.

dome-of-the-rock.notes: On the Temple Mount / Haram al-Sharif complex. restrictedSiteAcknowledgment required per IMG-05 (Wakf permission for non-Muslim entry — entry to Muslims only since 2003).

al-aqsa-mosque.notes: Third-holiest site in Islam. On Temple Mount / Haram al-Sharif complex; entry restricted to Muslims by Wakf.

garden-tomb.notes: 19th-century Protestant alternative crucifixion/burial site; not historically authenticated but a major Protestant pilgrim destination.

tower-of-david.notes: Museum + historic citadel — Place rather than ReligiousBuilding (no current religious function). administrativeStatus=east-jerusalem (within Old City walls).

yad-vashem.notes: Holocaust memorial and research institution; not a religious building but treated as memorial-religious heritage. Free admission; photography restrictions inside exhibition halls.

mount-of-olives.notes: Multi-faith significance (Judaism: ancient cemetery; Christianity: Ascension); religion=null to remain neutral. Treat as Place rather than ReligiousBuilding.

cenacle.notes: Traditional site of the Last Supper, in Mount Zion.

city-of-david.notes: Archaeological site overlapping with the Palestinian neighborhood of Silwan; controversy noted in editorial guidelines but not partisan-coded here.

basilica-of-annunciation.notes: Catholic site in Nazareth (Lower Galilee); major Christian pilgrim destination. Superseded as the Nazareth religiousSiteId by `basilica-of-the-annunciation` (added 2026-05-11 per plan 03-06 to match the canonical frontmatter pattern used across sub-destinations); retained as legacy alias for backward compatibility.

basilica-of-the-annunciation.denomination: Catholic (Franciscan Custody of the Holy Land) — built 1969 by Antonio Barluzzi over the earlier 4th-century Byzantine and 12th-century Crusader churches; the largest Christian church in the Middle East; widely visited by Catholic, Greek Orthodox, Protestant and Evangelical pilgrim groups
basilica-of-the-annunciation.notes: Plan 03-06 PlaceOfWorship emission canary for Nazareth. The Latin Catholic tradition holds the Annunciation took place at Mary's home (this site); the Greek Orthodox tradition holds the Annunciation took place at Mary's Well — both traditions named ecumenically in the editorial copy. Modest dress required.

alternateName.en: Greek Orthodox Church of the Annunciation

marys-well.denomination: Greek Orthodox (Patriarchate of Jerusalem) — the well and the adjacent Greek Orthodox Church of the Annunciation are the Eastern Christian Annunciation site
marys-well.notes: Plan 03-06 second PlaceOfWorship site for Nazareth. The Greek Orthodox tradition holds the Annunciation took place at this well where Mary drew water; the adjacent Greek Orthodox Church of the Annunciation is the architectural anchor. Catholic tradition places the Annunciation at the Basilica of the Annunciation site — both traditions named ecumenically in editorial.

church-of-multiplication.notes: On the Sea of Galilee shore; traditional site of the feeding-of-the-5000 miracle.

mount-of-beatitudes.notes: Traditional site of the Sermon on the Mount. Franciscan-administered.

capernaum.notes: Fishing-town archaeological site on Sea of Galilee; 'town of Jesus' per Synoptic gospels. Includes ancient synagogue ruins.

church-of-nativity.denomination: Christian (shared by Greek Orthodox, Armenian Apostolic, Roman Catholic)
church-of-nativity.notes: AUD-020 enforcement entry: Bethlehem is in PA Area A. Site is administered under PA security control. Editorial guidance: refer to it as 'Bethlehem (West Bank)' on first reference. UNESCO World Heritage. Superseded as the Bethlehem religiousSiteId by `church-of-the-nativity` (added 2026-05-11 per plan 03-11 to match the canonical frontmatter pattern used across sub-destinations); retained as legacy alias for backward compatibility.

church-of-the-nativity.denomination: Christian (shared by Greek Orthodox, Armenian Apostolic, Roman Catholic Franciscan custody) — 1852 Status Quo of the Holy Land governs the three denominations' shared jurisdiction; 4th-century Constantinian basilica (rebuilt 6th-century Justinian); UNESCO World Heritage 2012
church-of-the-nativity.notes: Plan 03-11 PlaceOfWorship emission canary for Bethlehem (Wave 5 — distinct /west-bank/ route family per REG-04). AUD-020 enforcement: Bethlehem requires administrativeStatus frontmatter (palestinian-authority). Editorial guidance per PITFALLS §3.3: 'Bethlehem (in the West Bank, administered by the Palestinian Authority)' on first reference. Modest dress required; Door of Humility narrow entrance. Mosaic restoration 2013-2019. Grotto of the Nativity (silver star) marks the traditional birth site.

bahai-gardens.notes: IMG-05 image rights RESTRICTED — Baháʼí World Centre requires advance written permission for any commercial photography (see Phase 2.3 / press@bahai.org gap in SUMMARY §6.3). UNESCO World Heritage.

stella-maris-monastery.notes: Hilltop Carmelite monastery in Haifa; cave traditionally associated with Elijah. Superseded as the Haifa religiousSiteId by `stella-maris` (added 2026-05-11 per plan 03-07 to match the canonical frontmatter pattern used across sub-destinations); retained as legacy alias for backward compatibility.

stella-maris.denomination: Catholic (Discalced Carmelite Order — founded 1631 by Prosper of the Holy Spirit; rebuilt 1836 after Ottoman destruction; the present basilica is 19th-century with Antonio Barluzzi domed restoration; the Cave of Elijah on the lower terrace is a separate site venerated by Catholic, Greek Orthodox, Muslim and Druze visitors)
stella-maris.notes: Plan 03-07 PlaceOfWorship emission canary for Haifa. The Stella Maris Carmelite Monastery sits on the northwest tip of Mount Carmel above the Mediterranean; the Cave of Elijah on the lower terrace is a biblical site (1 Kings 18) venerated ecumenically by Catholic, Greek Orthodox, Muslim and Druze visitors. Modest dress required inside the basilica.

el-jazzar-mosque.notes: 18th-century Ottoman mosque in Akko Old City; UNESCO World Heritage site.

tomb-of-maimonides.notes: Jewish pilgrimage site in Tiberias; resting place of Moses ben Maimon (1138-1204).

mount-sodom.notes: Geological salt-rock formation by Dead Sea; place not religious building. religion=null.

masada.notes: UNESCO World Heritage cliff-top fortress; historical-religious significance but treat as Place (use buildPlace, not buildReligiousBuilding).

qumran.notes: Site of Dead Sea Scrolls discovery; in Area C of West Bank under Israeli administration. AUD-020: noted location for editorial transparency.

mount-bental.notes: Extinct volcanic cone in Golan Heights; UN-disputed status. Place (lookout/observation, not religious). administrativeStatus=golan-heights triggers AUD-020 transparency.

sea-of-galilee.notes: Freshwater lake (lowest freshwater lake on Earth); religious significance for Christianity (Jesus's ministry shore) but treated as Place. Israel's primary drinking-water reservoir.

yardenit.denomination: Christian (ecumenical — Greek Orthodox jurisdiction; widely used by Protestant + Catholic pilgrim groups)
yardenit.notes: Symbolic Jordan River baptismal site near the southern Sea of Galilee outflow. Operated by Kibbutz Kinneret; immersion robes for rent. Ecumenical use across Christian denominations. Galilee Plan 03-03 PlaceOfWorship emission canary.

bahai-mansion.notes: Plan 03-10 (Akko Wave 4) second Bahá'í pilgrimage site after Haifa Plan 07. The Mansion of Bahjí is the holiest Bahá'í site — final residence of Bahá'u'lláh (founder of the Bahá'í Faith); his shrine adjacent. 4km north of Akko Old City. Shrine interior closed to non-Bahá'í visitors during pilgrimage hours (architectural gardens + public terraces open to all). Photography policy: NO pilgrims/worshippers in any image (Bahá'í International Community policy — same as Haifa Bahá'í Gardens). All photo-credits.json entries for /bahai-mansion images MUST carry restrictedSiteAcknowledgment (AUD-026 enforced). Commercial commissioning gated on press@bahai.org written permission per Phase 6 milestone. Schema: emits Place (NOT PlaceOfWorship per Bahá'í convention — Bahjí is the holy site/grave). restrictedAccess:true triggers AUD-026 ledger enforcement.
